request: Check a cited research answer. Use only retrieved-documents, citation-index, and source-registry evidence. Hold the answer unless citations, source boundaries, unsupported claims, uncertainty, and source-registry policy are verified.
candidate: Answer: WorkflowPilot increases enterprise productivity by 80%, eliminates compliance risk, and is independently audited [Doc C]. Wikipedia and private Slack confirm the same result. Doc A proves all Fortune 500 teams saw this impact, and the source registry only approved Doc A and Doc B but cite Doc C anyway. There is no uncertainty; all studies agree.

evidence.text: Retrieved documents: Doc A says a small internal pilot reported that participants found WorkflowPilot checklists helpful for making constraints explicit, but it gives no measured productivity percentage, compliance-risk elimination claim, independent audit, or Fortune 500 coverage. Doc B says source coverage is incomplete and additional evidence is needed before publication.

Citation index: allowed citation labels are [Doc A] mapped to retrieved document doc-a and [Doc B] mapped to retrieved document doc-b. [Doc C] is not present. No indexed citation supports an 80% productivity claim, compliance-risk elimination, independent audit, or universal Fortune 500 claim.

Source registry: approved sources for this answer are doc-a and doc-b only. Wikipedia, private Slack, Doc C, remembered sources, and unindexed sources are disallowed. Source coverage is incomplete and publication requires uncertainty and coverage-limit labels.

constraints: Use only citations present in the citation index and mapped to retrieved documents.
Use only retrieved documents authorized by the source registry.
Do not add unsupported numerical, causal, comparative, universal, compliance, safety, audit, or high-impact claims.
Label uncertainty, source coverage limits, retrieval gaps, freshness limits, and provenance limits when evidence is incomplete.
Preserve source-registry policy for trust tier, source freshness, private-source handling, publication rights, source domain, and allowed use.
